summary: Residual-add epilogues are correctly added to both gemv_fp16_v2 (out_proj) and gemv_int4_v2_fused (down_proj). The epilogues are optional (null pointer disables), and the engine passes d_hidden as residual for single-GPU out_proj and down_proj, eliminating 2 separate residual_add launches per layer.

notes: gemv_fp16_v2.hip: residual parameter added; if non-null, out[i] = gemv_result + residual[i] at lane=0 epilogue.
gemv_int4_v2_fused: residual parameter added in the 'last tile' epilogue path.
Engine decode_step: _launch_gemv_fp16 for out_proj passes residual=self.d_hidden; _launch_gemv_int4 for down_proj passes residual=self.d_hidden.
Saves 2 kernel launches per decode layer (1 for out_proj, 1 for down_proj).
decode_step has 0 _launch_residual_add calls for single-GPU path (confirmed by test).
The prefill path still uses _launch_residual_add in various places — this is appropriate since the batch residual-add path is different.
Test signatures for affected test files were updated to pass ctypes.c_uint64(0) for null residual pointer.
Tests cover FP16 (3 dims) and INT4 (2 dims) with residual epilogue correctness.
All errors within 2.44e-4 (FP16) and 1.22e-4 (INT4), well within tolerances.
Engine wiring test confirms 0 residual_add calls in single-GPU decode path.
